seo.title: Haystack Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Haystack agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Haystack pipelines for resume screening and candidate ranking face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act, which classify hiring AI as high-risk. Pipeline failures such as retriever irrelevance or generator hallucinations can lead to discriminatory outcomes based on protected characteristics. Vex provides essential guardrails to monitor, audit, and mitigate these risks in real-time.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability; annual bias audits required.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors pipeline outputs for biased language or scoring patterns, flagging disparities in candidate rankings by protected attributes.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits on automated employment decision tools.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs AI usage in every pipeline run and generates audit-ready reports on decision criteria and demographic impacts.

complianceRequirements.description: High-risk AI systems in employment must ensure transparency, human oversight, and ongoing risk management with documented compliance.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides runtime observability, traces decisions back to retrieved contexts, and alerts on hallucinated or unsupported recommendations.

complianceRequirements.description: Resume screening must accommodate disabilities and avoid disparate impact on protected groups; notices required for AI usage.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex evaluates outputs for accessibility language and flags decisions lacking evidence from retrieved resumes.

industryRisks.name: Discriminatory Candidate Ranking
industryRisks.description: Retriever returns resumes biased toward certain demographics, amplified by generator scoring.
industryRisks.example: Pipeline ranks male candidates higher due to keyword matches from outdated job descriptions, violating EEOC rules.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Rejection Reasons
industryRisks.description: Generator fabricates reasons for rejection beyond retrieved context, creating audit trail issues.
industryRisks.example: AI rejects candidate claiming 'lack of leadership experience' not present in resume, triggering lawsuit.

industryRisks.name: Silent Component Failures
industryRisks.description: Custom components fail without logging, hiding bias detection or notification logic errors.
industryRisks.example: Bias audit component crashes, allowing unmonitored discriminatory screening for 3 months.

industryRisks.name: Type Mismatch in Scoring
industryRisks.description: Pipeline components pass incompatible data types, corrupting diversity metrics calculations.
industryRisks.example: String scores treated as numbers crash EEOC compliance dashboard.

implementation.title: Add Vex Guardrails Component to Haystack Pipeline
implementation.description: Insert Vex as a custom component after retriever and generator to monitor for bias and compliance violations.

implementation.title: Monitor Output Type Safety and Hallucinations
implementation.description: Use Vex to validate component outputs match expected schemas and flag hallucinations in candidate assessments.

implementation.title: EEOC Bias Audit Logging
implementation.description: Automatically log all decisions with demographic signals for annual compliance audits.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Haystack in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A Fortune 500 company deploys a Haystack pipeline for resume screening, using an InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever to match candidates against job descriptions and an OpenAIGenerator to rank and explain recommendations. The pipeline processes 10,000+ resumes monthly across engineering, sales, and marketing roles. Initially, the system performs well on precision but begins showing patterns: female candidates for technical roles receive 15% lower scores on average, and candidates over 50 are rarely shortlisted despite qualifications.

The HR team discovers the issue only after a rejected candidate files an EEOC complaint, citing disparate impact. Investigation reveals the retriever favors resumes with 'startup' and 'scale-up' keywords correlated with younger demographics, while the generator hallucinates 'cultural fit' concerns not grounded in retrieved context. Without observability, the company faces a class-action lawsuit threat and mandatory system decommissioning.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex as a pipeline component, the company gains full visibility into failure modes. Vex flagged retriever bias within the first week, showing 23% of documents contained age-correlated terms. Hallucination detection blocked 17% of generator outputs lacking context evidence. Automated EEOC audit logs enabled quick remediation: retriever re-training and prompt engineering reduced disparities to <3%. The system passed annual bias audit, avoided $2.3M in potential fines, and improved diverse hire rates by 28%.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Haystack pipeline today to eliminate bias risks and ensure EEOC/NYC Law 144 compliance.
